overallMatch.summary: Strong fit. Jeffrey has 15+ years of enterprise SaaS sales in Greater China, 3+ years leading teams with strong quota attainment (118–143%), and fluency in English and Cantonese. He is Hong Kong–based and has sold Workday HCM/Finance SaaS to major enterprises. Gaps are mainly around explicit MEDDPICC/Salesforce mentions and direct XM/VoC experience; these are addressable in the interview.

granularScores.rationale: Strong SaaS, enterprise sales, complex deal cycles, and leadership. MEDDPICC and Salesforce not stated but likely used at Workday. XM/VoC is adjacent to HCM/HR transformation.
15+ years enterprise sales, 3+ years managing teams, consistent over-attainment (118–143% team, 300%+ individual). Workday/Oracle/Microsoft SaaS background.
Bachelor of Accounting meets typical requirements. No MBA or sales certifications; experience compensates.
Hong Kong SAR, Greater China focus, and Mandarin fluency support Hong Kong + Taiwan coverage.
Career progression from Infor/Oracle to Microsoft to Workday; moved from IC to RSD; HR/Finance SaaS across multiple vendors.
Head of Sales aligns with his trajectory. Qualtrics APAC expansion and Silver Lake ownership offer growth.
Workday RSD likely in a similar band. No explicit comp data; assume reasonable fit with room to negotiate.

jobRequirements.evidence: 15+ years; Workday AE 2015–2023 (8 years), Oracle, Microsoft, Infor
RSD Feb 2023–Present (3+ years); managed 3 AEs; 118–143% team attainment
Workday, Oracle HCM Cloud/Fusion/Taleo, Microsoft Dynamics; Deep expertise in SaaS
Complex Sales Cycle Management, Solution Selling; methodology not named
Not stated; Workday typically uses Salesforce
Enterprise deals (AIA, HKEX, Tencent, etc.); Complex Sales Cycle Management
143%, 128%, 118% FY24–FY26; metric based skill development implied
Not stated; Greater China role implies travel

strengths: Quota attainment – Team attainment 118%, 128%, 143% FY24–FY26; individual 300%+ in multiple years; Top AE APJ FY19, FY23; Top Financial Contributor APJ FY18
Greater China SaaS experience – Workday RSD (Greater China), Oracle HCM Cloud, Microsoft Dynamics AX; strong enterprise deal list (AIA, HKEX, Tencent, Bytedance, NIO, etc.)
Language and location – Cantonese (native), English (fluent), Mandarin (fluent); Hong Kong–based; ideal for Hong Kong + Taiwan
Leadership and team management – 3+ years managing AEs; clear record of building and leading teams to over-attainment
Complex enterprise sales – Long-cycle HR/Finance transformations; experience with large, complex contracts and negotiations
Brand alignment – Workday and Qualtrics both enterprise SaaS; Qualtrics XM complements HR/employee experience

gaps.recommendation: State explicitly if used at Workday; if not, describe a similar qualification framework (Challenger, SPIN, etc.) and how you qualify and close deals.
Confirm use at Workday; describe CRM hygiene, forecasting, pipeline management, and compliance metrics.
Emphasize employee experience and feedback in HR transformation; connect Workday's people analytics to Qualtrics' XM and VoC.
Clarify any Taiwan deals or relationships; show how you would build presence there.
Confirm willingness to travel 1–2x/month within Greater China.

interviewPrepFocus: MEDDPICC and Salesforce – Prepare concrete examples of how you qualify opportunities, run pipeline reviews, and use CRM for forecasting and compliance
XM and VoC positioning – Learn Qualtrics' XM/VoC story and link it to your HR/Finance transformation experience and employee experience work
Taiwan strategy – Outline a 90-day plan for Hong Kong and Taiwan: target accounts, partners, and coverage model
Business management – Prepare examples of P&L thinking, territory planning, and resource allocation

highlights.executiveSummary: Qualtrics is a category-defining Experience Management (XM) platform serving 20,000+ brands across 100+ countries. Founded 2002, acquired by SAP ($8B), IPO 2021, $12.5B Silver Lake buyout 2023. Privately held with strong AI investment.
highlights.leadership: New CEO Jason Maynard (Feb 2026), ex-Oracle/NetSuite, strong enterprise sales background
highlights.products: XM platform (CX, EX, product, brand); Gartner VoC leader; AI roadmap
highlights.apacExpansion: ~1,200 hires planned; Singapore data centre; Center for Experience Management in Singapore
highlights.hongKongPresence: Target market; XM Live events; Country Manager role may be filled
highlights.culture: TACOS values; Glassdoor 3.5/5, 57% recommend; sales quotas described as demanding
highlights.recommendation: Proceed with caution – Strong category position and APAC expansion, but Hong Kong presence is less established than Singapore/Tokyo, and Glassdoor scores (especially senior management at 2.6/5) suggest due diligence.
